nodes.label: electron-builder Extra Resources Config
nodes.rationale: All runtime files placed outside asar in resources/ so they are accessible via process.resourcesPath in main process and forked server; write-able and runtime-required files must not be inside the read-only asar

nodes.label: electron-builder Selected Over electron-forge
nodes.rationale: electron-builder is a drop-in that reads existing dist/ output without restructuring; electron-forge requires migrating to its own build pipeline which conflicts with Nx targets; electron-builder is the dominant pattern in Nx+Electron monorepos; configured via single electron-builder.yml alongside project.json

nodes.label: Asar Bundle Architecture (Inside vs Outside)
outside_asar_in_resources: prisma/migrations/ - prisma migrate deploy reads SQL at runtime
Prisma query engine binary - native binary must not be in asar
SQLite dms.db at app.getPath(userData) - created at runtime
nodes.rationale: Files inside the asar are read-only; the SQLite .db file is created fresh in userData on first launch via prisma migrate deploy and must never be inside the asar

nodes.label: DATABASE_URL Strategy via Electron userData
nodes.implementation: Set DATABASE_URL on process.env in Electron main process before startServer() forks Fastify; forked child inherits it automatically via spread of process.env
nodes.rationale: DATABASE_URL must be set before server fork so child inherits it; server's initializeDatabaseUrl() already reads process.env.DATABASE_URL as non-AWS fallback

nodes.label: Prisma Migration Strategy in Packaged App
nodes.timing: Migrations run in Electron main process before startServer() forks Fastify
nodes.invocation: execFileSync with bundled prisma binary from process.resourcesPath; no node_modules in packaged app
nodes.rationale: Running migrations before server fork guarantees schema is current before any API requests are served; first launch creates dms.db fresh via migrations without needing a seed DB copy
nodes.norm_label: prisma migration strategy in packaged app

nodes.label: Electron Main Process Startup Sequence
steps: wait for server ready IPC message

nodes.label: BrowserWindow Security Configuration
nodes.rationale: nodeIntegration:false keeps Node APIs out of renderer; contextIsolation:true isolates preload bridge from web page; sandbox:true adds additional renderer isolation layer

nodes.label: Angular Router Navigation Handling in Electron
nodes.behavior: Same-origin localhost URLs treated as internal; external URLs prevented; window.open same-origin redirected to existing window; new window creation always denied
nodes.norm_label: angular router navigation handling in electron

nodes.label: BaseTable Old Imperative API (Deprecated)
deprecated_patterns: initDataSource() method with RxJS Observable-based loadFn
manual refresh() calls after signal/state changes
manual sort handler that calls refreshTable()
nodes.rationale: Imperative pattern incompatible with SmartNgRX signals; data source disconnected from signals; required manual lifecycle management

nodes.label: BaseTable New Signal-Based API
new_patterns: automatic reactivity - no manual refresh needed

nodes.label: BaseTable Built-in Signal-Based Sorting
nodes.rationale: Sorting internalized to remove need for host component to manage sort state and call refresh

fields.field: string - property name in data object

inputs.data: T[] - required, array of table data (must be signal)

nodes.label: E2E Shared SQLite Database Isolation Issue
nodes.problem: Chromium and Firefox share single test-database.db; parallel execution causes SQLite locking and data pollution
nodes.cause: Default Playwright parallel execution interleaves tests between browsers against same DB
nodes.norm_label: e2e shared sqlite database isolation issue

nodes.label: E2E Sequential Browser Execution Strategy
nodes.solution: Run browsers sequentially (complete one browser before starting the next) via separate pnpm scripts
future_improvements: beforeEach/afterEach hooks to reset DB state
Separate backend instances on different ports

nodes.label: Invalid CSV Test Fixture (fidelity-not-a-csv.txt)
nodes.content: Plain text file that should be rejected by the import process
nodes.purpose: Negative test fixture to verify CSV import validation rejects non-CSV files
